rule.description: Prefer Angular standalone components, directives, and pipes for new work. Keep legacy NgModule usage only when a dependency requires it or migration cost is not justified.
Organize code by feature and layer. Prefer folders such as core/, features/, shared/, and testing/ over generic utility dumping grounds. Keep scanning logic outside view components.

Keep reusable interfaces, type aliases, and DTO-like contracts in dedicated .types.ts files inside the closest feature or shared domain folder. Avoid large inline type declarations inside Angular components and services.
Keep Angular components focused on presentation, bindings, and interaction wiring. Pure scanning, replacement, storage, and serialization logic belongs in services or pure utilities.
Prefer Angular control flow and iterable rendering for repeated UI structures. Do not hardcode repeated option blocks, action lists, or toggle rows when an array-driven template is clearer.
Use ChangeDetectionStrategy.OnPush by default for standalone components. State updates should be explicit and predictable.
Prefer Angular signals or focused RxJS streams for shared UI state. Do not store cross-feature mutable state directly in components or in DOM attributes.
Use RxJS for asynchronous streams, IO, and event orchestration. Prefer pure synchronous functions for deterministic masking and token matching.
Do not build primary application behavior with manual DOM querying or imperative node insertion. Angular templates should render scan results, toggles, and outputs from structured state.
Move rule catalogs, static labels, storage keys, and configuration maps into dedicated .constants.ts files. Avoid embedding long readonly dictionaries inside components.

The masking engine must expose deterministic pure functions for: token detection, match normalization, mask generation, and masked-output assembly. UI code may consume these functions but must not redefine them.

Sort import declarations alphabetically by module path and sort named specifiers alphabetically inside braces. Keep Angular framework imports grouped first, then third-party imports, then local imports.
Prefer ECMAScript private fields (#field) for internal service and utility state when privacy matters. Keep public APIs explicit.
Mark public methods and accessors explicitly when they are part of the service or class API. This improves grepability during refactors.
For single-statement conditionals or loops, braces may be omitted when readability is not harmed. Add braces immediately when nesting or side effects increase ambiguity.
Wrap async UI actions, storage calls, clipboard operations, and serialization paths in defensive error handling. Failure modes should degrade safely without exposing raw sensitive output.
Centralize browser storage keys and URL query keys in constants modules. Never duplicate raw storage key strings across services and components.
Keep Jest unit tests close to the source they validate using *.spec.ts. Use Playwright only for user-critical end-to-end flows, not as a replacement for engine unit coverage.
Regex rules must be grouped by confidence and domain, accompanied by fixture tests, and consumed through fresh instances or stateless matching helpers to avoid mutable lastIndex bugs.
The primary copy and export actions must always operate on the currently masked output. If the masked output is stale or unavailable, the UI must block the action and explain why.
